title: platform-user — compliance evidence
description: SOC 2 HEAVY. This module is the substrate for every internal-workforce control that a Type II attestation covers — CC6.1 logical access, CC6.7 encryption + credential mgmt, CC7.2 change monitoring, CC7.3 incident response, CC8.1 change management. Every state transition on a PlatformUser writes an audit row; every PII access writes a meta-audit row; every offboarding is measured against a 24h access-removal SLA. Distinct from the tenant plane's compliance surface (which is GDPR-heavy) — platform staff are Stackra's own employees, so tenant-facing GDPR articles like Art. 17 (right to erasure) do NOT govern PlatformUser rows.

controls.evidence: Every route on the platform_admin guard is permission-gated via PlatformUserPolicy + PlatformProfilePolicy. Every PII-unmute ability (viewPii, viewHrContext, viewOnCallPhone) writes an audit meta-row. Freshly-hired PlatformUsers land with least-privilege — only `platform.platform_profiles.view.self` + `.update.self` — additional roles granted post-hire by super_admin, itself audit-logged.

`platform-user:hire` command + `POST /platform-users` route enforce the MFA-on-Identity gate + a super_admin actor + a documented reason. Rejects a hire whose Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted — platform staff MUST be MFA-enrolled before receiving platform_admin credentials.

PlatformUserManagerReassigned + PlatformUserDepartmentChanged + role assignment events all fire on afterCommit + write audit rows. The `platform-user:audit-report` command aggregates all modifications for a compliance-review pass.

24h SLA on RevokePlatformUserAccessJob. Access revocation covers Sanctum PATs (via personal_access_tokens delete), JWTs (via jti deny-list cache), active impersonation sessions (via TerminateImpersonation), and on-call rotation membership (via OnCallResolver::unregister). PagerDuty alerts fire at 12h (half-SLA).

`platform.audit` middleware writes an audit row for EVERY platform-plane request that mutates state, tagged with a correlation_id shared across the request lifecycle. Read-only requests write a lighter activity_log row when they touch PII (via `platform_user.pii_access.total` metric).

PlatformUserSuspended is a synchronous access-pause primitive used during Sev-1 security incidents. On-call rotation resolver (backed by PagerDuty) routes escalations to the currently-on-duty PlatformUser. The `platform.security_review` entitlement gates access to session-revocation-across-the-fleet.
PlatformUserRestored reverses a suspend. Retention-hold window (90 days) allows an accidentally-offboarded PlatformUser to be manually restored via a super_admin's compliance-officer-signed request.

Every platform-plane audit row is retained in HOT storage for 365 days (config('platform-user.audit.retention_hot_days')) — the SOC 2 attestation window. Post-hot, rotates to cold via audit::RotateAuditColdStorageJob for 7y retention.
PlatformUserPolicy + PlatformProfilePolicy define the ability catalogue. Grants are per-user via `platform_role_assignments` + `platform_entitlement_grants` (both Wave 1b).

PlatformUser is a projection of Identity into the staff plane — the identity_id FK is the SINGLE root of trust. MFA enrolment gate at hire time + runtime middleware enforcement together guarantee no non-MFA platform_admin sessions.

PII on PlatformProfile is redacted by default; unmute abilities (viewPii, viewHrContext) each write a meta-audit row. The offboarding_reason field is redacted even for super_admin (visible only under `viewHrContext` which is HR + legal only).
Credentials + MFA secrets live on Identity (KMS-encrypted). PlatformUser stores no credential material of its own. See identity::compliance for the KMS envelope-encryption evidence.

gdpr.scope_note: PlatformUser + PlatformProfile rows carry PII (name, phone, email via Identity), so GDPR data-processing principles apply — but Stackra is the DATA CONTROLLER for its own employee records, and processing basis is the employment contract (Art. 6(1)(b)). Data-subject rights apply, though Art. 17 (erasure) is bounded by the audit-retention obligations under Art. 30.

controls.evidence: PII on PlatformProfile classified `restricted` (data-classes.json). Redactor rules apply unless the caller carries the explicit view.pii permission. Every unmute writes a meta-audit row.
Every PlatformUser row is created under the employment contract with the referenced Identity's real human. Consent framework does not apply to internal-workforce processing.

PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob hard-deletes the row after the 90-day retention hold. Audit rows referencing the ex-PlatformUser survive with an anonymised actor placeholder (SOC 2 evidence retention prevails over erasure for the employment record). Compliance officer + DPO co-sign any manual pre-90-day purge (rare).
audit-heavy trail (see CC7.2). PlatformUser mutations are the internal-workforce records-of-processing evidence for the Stackra DPO.

hr_compliance.scope_note: Regional labour-law compliance (EEOC in the US, GDPR-adjacent in the EU) governs employment-record retention + access. This module surfaces the hooks; the retention policy is jurisdiction-dependent + set per Stackra workplace, not per PlatformUser.

controls.evidence: The 90-day retention hold + subsequent hard delete of the PlatformUser row is compatible with the 3-year US EEOC record-retention floor because the AUDIT trail (which lives on the audit module) persists 7y. HR gets a redacted export via `platform-user:audit-report --format=pdf` before hard-delete for their own separate archive.
`platform.security_review` entitlement grants require a co-signer from the security department. Documented via requires_co_signer=true on the entitlement definition.

regime_tier_summary.note: SOC 2 Type II is the load-bearing framework here — the module is designed around its criteria. ISO 27001 controls are naturally covered as a consequence. GDPR + HR-compliance apply narrowly (PII handling + employment-record retention).
